IuliaBreakingNews package¶

    • Submodules

      • Migration
        • Migration.dependencies
        • Migration.initial
        • Migration.operations
    • Module contents

Submodules¶

IuliaBreakingNews.admin module¶

Admin configuration for IuliaBreakingNews models. Registers CustomUser, Article, and Newsletter with the admin site. Publisher is now a role on CustomUser, not a separate model.

class IuliaBreakingNews.admin.ArticleAdmin(model, admin_site)¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin view for Article model.

actions = ['approve_articles']¶
approve_articles(request, queryset)¶

Bulk approve selected articles.

list_display = ('title', 'author', 'publisher', 'approved', 'created_at')¶
list_editable = ('approved',)¶
list_filter = ('approved', 'publisher', 'created_at')¶
property media¶
ordering = ('-created_at',)¶
search_fields = ('title', 'author__username')¶
class IuliaBreakingNews.admin.CustomUserAdmin(model, admin_site)¶

Bases: UserAdmin

Admin view for CustomUser model.

add_fieldsets = ((None, {'classes': ('wide',), 'fields': ('username', 'usable_password', 'password1', 'password2')}), ('Role', {'fields': ('role',)}))¶
fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), ('Role & Subscriptions', {'fields': ('role', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists', 'journalists', 'editors')}))¶
list_display = ('username', 'email', 'role', 'is_staff', 'is_active')¶
list_filter = ('role', 'is_staff', 'is_active')¶
property media¶
ordering = ('username',)¶
search_fields = ('username', 'email')¶
class IuliaBreakingNews.admin.NewsletterAdmin(model, admin_site)¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin view for Newsletter model.

filter_horizontal = ('articles',)¶
list_display = ('title', 'author', 'created_at')¶
property media¶
ordering = ('-created_at',)¶
search_fields = ('title', 'author__username')¶

IuliaBreakingNews.api_urls module¶

URL patterns for the IuliaBreakingNews REST API. All routes are prefixed with /api/ in the main urls.py.

IuliaBreakingNews.api_views module¶

RESTful API views for IuliaBreakingNews. Uses DRF Token Authentication and role-based permissions.

class IuliaBreakingNews.api_views.ApproveArticleAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: APIView

POST /api/articles/<id>/approve/ Approve an article (editors only).

authentication_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication'>]¶
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
post(request, pk)¶

Approve an article.

class IuliaBreakingNews.api_views.ApprovedArticleLogAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: APIView

POST /api/approved/ Receives and logs approved article data posted by the signal. No authentication required (internal use only).

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.AllowAny'>]¶
post(request)¶

Log an approved article posted by the signal.

class IuliaBreakingNews.api_views.ArticleDetailAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: APIView

GET /api/articles/<id>/ — Retrieve single article. PUT /api/articles/<id>/ — Update (editors/journalists). DELETE /api/articles/<id>/ — Delete (editors/journalists).

authentication_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication'>]¶
delete(request, pk)¶

Delete an article (editors and journalists only).

get(request, pk)¶

Retrieve a single approved article.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
put(request, pk)¶

Update an article (editors and journalists only).

class IuliaBreakingNews.api_views.ArticleListAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: APIView

GET /api/articles/ — List all approved articles. POST /api/articles/ — Create a new article (journalists only).

authentication_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication'>]¶
get(request)¶

Return all approved articles.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
post(request)¶

Create a new article (journalists only).

class IuliaBreakingNews.api_views.ArticleSubscribedAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: APIView

GET /api/articles/subscribed/ Returns articles from the reader’s subscribed publishers/journalists.

authentication_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication'>]¶
get(request)¶

Return articles from subscribed sources.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
class IuliaBreakingNews.api_views.LoginAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: APIView

POST /api/login/ Returns a token for valid credentials.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.AllowAny'>]¶
post(request)¶

Authenticate user and return token.

class IuliaBreakingNews.api_views.LogoutAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: APIView

POST /api/logout/ Deletes the user’s token.

authentication_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication'>]¶
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
post(request)¶

Delete token to logout.

class IuliaBreakingNews.api_views.NewsletterListAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: APIView

GET /api/newsletters/ — List all newsletters. POST /api/newsletters/ — Create newsletter (journalists only).

authentication_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication'>]¶
get(request)¶

Return all newsletters.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
post(request)¶

Create a newsletter (journalists only).

class IuliaBreakingNews.api_views.PublisherDetailAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: APIView

GET /api/publishers/<id>/ Returns a single publisher with their team.

authentication_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication'>]¶
get(request, pk)¶

Return a single publisher.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
class IuliaBreakingNews.api_views.PublisherJoinAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: APIView

POST /api/publishers/<id>/join/ Journalist or editor joins a publisher’s team.

authentication_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication'>]¶
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
post(request, pk)¶

Join a publisher’s team.

class IuliaBreakingNews.api_views.PublisherLeaveAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: APIView

POST /api/publishers/<id>/leave/ Journalist or editor leaves a publisher’s team.

authentication_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication'>]¶
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
post(request, pk)¶

Leave a publisher’s team.

class IuliaBreakingNews.api_views.PublisherListAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: APIView

GET /api/publishers/ Returns a list of all publishers.

authentication_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication'>]¶
get(request)¶

Return all publishers.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
class IuliaBreakingNews.api_views.PublisherTeamAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: APIView

GET /api/publishers/<id>/team/

Returns the publisher’s journalists and editors.

POST /api/publishers/<id>/team/

Publisher updates their team (adds/removes members).

authentication_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication'>]¶
get(request, pk)¶

Return the publisher’s team.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
post(request, pk)¶

Update the publisher’s team. Only the publisher themselves can update their team.

IuliaBreakingNews.apps module¶

Application configuration for IuliaBreakingNews. Registers signals when the app is ready. Groups and permissions are created automatically after migrations via the post_migrate signal — no manual setup required.

class IuliaBreakingNews.apps.IuliaBreakingNewsConfig(app_name, app_module)¶

Bases: AppConfig

Configuration for the IuliaBreakingNews app.

default_auto_field = 'django.db.models.BigAutoField'¶
name = 'IuliaBreakingNews'¶
ready()¶

Import signals when the app is ready. The import is intentionally inside ready() to avoid circular imports — this is the standard Django pattern for registering signals.

IuliaBreakingNews.decorators module¶

Custom decorators for role-based access control. Used in views to restrict access based on the user’s role field instead of relying on Django’s permission_required decorator.

Usage:

@login_required @journalist_required def my_view(request):

…

IuliaBreakingNews.decorators.editor_or_journalist_required(view_func)¶

Decorator that restricts access to users with ‘editor’ or ‘journalist’ roles.

IuliaBreakingNews.decorators.editor_required(view_func)¶

Decorator that restricts access to users with the ‘editor’ role. Returns 403 for other roles.

IuliaBreakingNews.decorators.journalist_required(view_func)¶

Decorator that restricts access to users with the ‘journalist’ role. Returns 403 for other roles.

IuliaBreakingNews.decorators.publisher_required(view_func)¶

Decorator that restricts access to users with the ‘publisher’ role.

IuliaBreakingNews.forms module¶

Forms for the IuliaBreakingNews application. Handles registration, article/newsletter creation, and publisher team management.

All forms include custom validation methods (clean_*) to enforce business rules before saving to the database.

class IuliaBreakingNews.forms.ArticleForm(*args, user=None, **kwargs)¶

Bases: ModelForm

Form for creating and editing articles with validation.

class Meta¶

Bases: object

Meta options for ArticleForm.

fields = ('title', 'content', 'publisher')¶
model¶

alias of Article

widgets = {'content': <django.forms.widgets.Textarea object>, 'publisher': <django.forms.widgets.Select object>, 'title': <django.forms.widgets.TextInput object>}¶
base_fields = {'content': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'publisher': <django.forms.models.ModelChoiceField object>, 'title': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
clean_content()¶

Validate article content length.

clean_title()¶

Validate article title length.

declared_fields = {}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

Validate that only approved articles are included.

Form for publishers to manage their team. Allows selecting journalists and editors.

Registration form with role selection and validation.

alias of CustomUser

clean_email()¶

Validate that email is not already in use.

clean_role()¶

Validate that the selected role is valid.

clean_username()¶

Validate that username is long enough.

declared_fields = {'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

save(commit=True)¶

Save user and trigger group assignment.

IuliaBreakingNews.models module¶

models.py - IuliaBreakingNews

Defines the database schema for the IuliaBreakingNews application. All models inherit from Django’s base Model class.

Models:

  • CustomUser: Extended user model with role-based fields.

  • Article: Represents a news article written by a journalist.

  • Newsletter: A curated collection of approved articles.

class IuliaBreakingNews.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: Model

A news article written by a journalist. Can be independent or published under a publisher. The publisher field links to a CustomUser with role=’publisher’.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

approved¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

author¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id¶
content¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
id¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

newsletters¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>¶
publisher¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

publisher_id¶
title¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class IuliaBreakingNews.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: AbstractUser

Extended user model with role-based fields.

Roles: - reader: can view articles/newsletters, subscribe to

journalists and publishers.

  • journalist: can write articles and newsletters, works with multiple publishers.

  • editor: can approve/delete articles and newsletters, works under a publisher.

  • publisher: manages a team of journalists and editors.

Field separation: - Reader fields (subscriptions) are cleared for non-readers. - Journalist/Editor fields (publisher team) are cleared

for non-journalists and non-editors.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

ROLE_CHOICES = [('reader', 'Reader'), ('journalist', 'Journalist'), ('editor', 'Editor'), ('publisher', 'Publisher')]¶
articles¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

auth_token¶

Accessor to the related object on the reverse side of a one-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Restaurant(Model):
    place = OneToOneField(Place, related_name='restaurant')

Place.restaurant is a ReverseOneToOneDescriptor instance.

date_joined¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

editors¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
get_publishers()¶

Return all publishers this journalist/editor belongs to. Works via reverse relation from publisher’s team fields.

get_role_display(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.CharField: role>)¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

id¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

is_active¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

property is_editor¶

Return True if user is an editor.

property is_journalist¶

Return True if user is a journalist.

property is_publisher¶

Return True if user is a publisher.

property is_reader¶

Return True if user is a reader.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

A curated collection of articles created by a journalist.

IuliaBreakingNews.permissions module¶

Custom DRF permissions based on user role.

class IuliaBreakingNews.permissions.IsEditor¶

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access only to users with the Editor role.

has_permission(request, view)¶

Check if user is authenticated and has Editor role.

message = 'Only editors can access this endpoint.'¶
class IuliaBreakingNews.permissions.IsEditorOrJournalist¶

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access to editors and journalists.

has_permission(request, view)¶

Check if user has Editor or Journalist role.

message = 'Only editors or journalists can access this endpoint.'¶
class IuliaBreakingNews.permissions.IsJournalist¶

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access only to users with the Journalist role.

has_permission(request, view)¶

Check if user is authenticated and has Journalist role.

message = 'Only journalists can access this endpoint.'¶
class IuliaBreakingNews.permissions.IsPublisher¶

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access only to users with the Publisher role.

has_permission(request, view)¶

Check if user is authenticated and has Publisher role.

message = 'Only publishers can access this endpoint.'¶
class IuliaBreakingNews.permissions.IsReader¶

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access only to users with the Reader role.

has_permission(request, view)¶

Check if user is authenticated and has Reader role.

message = 'Only readers can access this endpoint.'¶

IuliaBreakingNews.serializers module¶

DRF serializers for Article, Newsletter, and CustomUser. Publisher is a CustomUser with role=’publisher’.

class IuliaBreakingNews.serializers.ApprovedArticleLogSerializer(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: Serializer

Serializer for the /api/approved/ endpoint. Logs approved article data posted by the signal. Read-only — create and update are not needed.

create(validated_data)¶

Not implemented — this serializer is read-only.

update(instance, validated_data)¶

Not implemented — this serializer is read-only.

class IuliaBreakingNews.serializers.ArticleSerializer(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for Article model.

class Meta¶

Bases: object

Meta options for ArticleSerializer.

fields = ('id', 'title', 'content', 'author', 'publisher', 'publisher_id', 'approved', 'created_at')¶
model¶

alias of Article

read_only_fields = ('id', 'author', 'approved', 'created_at')¶
class IuliaBreakingNews.serializers.CustomUserSerializer(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for CustomUser model (read-only public info).

class Meta¶

Bases: object

Meta options for CustomUserSerializer.

fields = ('id', 'username', 'email', 'role')¶
model¶

alias of CustomUser

read_only_fields = ('id', 'role')¶
class IuliaBreakingNews.serializers.NewsletterSerializer(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for Newsletter model.

class Meta¶

Bases: object

Meta options for NewsletterSerializer.

fields = ('id', 'title', 'description', 'author', 'articles', 'article_ids', 'created_at')¶
model¶

alias of Newsletter

read_only_fields = ('id', 'author', 'created_at')¶
class IuliaBreakingNews.serializers.PublisherSerializer(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for Publisher profile. A Publisher is a CustomUser with role=’publisher’. Includes team counts and member lists.

class Meta¶

Bases: object

Meta options for PublisherSerializer.

fields = ('id', 'username', 'email', 'journalist_count', 'editor_count', 'journalist_names', 'editor_names')¶
model¶

alias of CustomUser

read_only_fields = ('id',)¶
get_editor_count(obj)¶

Return number of editors in the team.

get_editor_names(obj)¶

Return list of editor usernames.

get_journalist_count(obj)¶

Return number of journalists in the team.

get_journalist_names(obj)¶

Return list of journalist usernames.

class IuliaBreakingNews.serializers.PublisherTeamSerializer(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for updating a publisher’s team. Accepts journalist_ids and editor_ids for team management.

class Meta¶

Bases: object

Meta options for PublisherTeamSerializer.

fields = ('id', 'username', 'journalists', 'journalist_ids', 'editors', 'editor_ids')¶
model¶

alias of CustomUser

read_only_fields = ('id', 'username')¶
update(instance, validated_data)¶

Update publisher’s team members.

IuliaBreakingNews.setup module¶

Automatic setup for IuliaBreakingNews groups and permissions. Called via post_migrate signal after every migration run. No manual commands required.

IuliaBreakingNews.setup.create_groups_and_permissions(sender, **kwargs)¶

Create user groups and assign permissions automatically after migrations are applied.

Groups created: - Reader: view articles and newsletters - Editor: view, change, delete articles and newsletters - Journalist: add, view, change, delete articles and newsletters - Publisher: view articles and newsletters

IuliaBreakingNews.signals module¶

Django signals for IuliaBreakingNews. Triggered after an article is saved and approved:

  1. Sends email to all subscribers of the journalist or publisher.

  2. POSTs the approved article to /api/approved/ endpoint.

IuliaBreakingNews.signals.on_article_approved(sender, instance, **kwargs)¶

Fires after every Article save. Only acts when the article has just been approved. The sender argument is required by Django’s signal API.

IuliaBreakingNews.tests module¶

Unit tests for IuliaBreakingNews RESTful API. Covers authentication, role-based access, subscriptions, publisher team management, article/newsletter CRUD, and signal logic.

class IuliaBreakingNews.tests.AuthenticationTests(methodName='runTest')¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for login and token authentication.

setUp()¶

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_authenticated_request_to_articles_returns_200()¶

Authenticated requests should succeed.

test_login_with_invalid_credentials_returns_401()¶

An invalid login should return 401.

test_login_with_valid_credentials_returns_token()¶

A valid login should return a token.

test_unauthenticated_request_to_articles_returns_401()¶

Unauthenticated requests should be rejected.

class IuliaBreakingNews.tests.NewsletterTests(methodName='runTest')¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for newsletter creation and access.

setUp()¶

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_all_roles_can_view_newsletters()¶

All authenticated users should be able to view newsletters.

test_journalist_can_create_newsletter()¶

Journalists should be able to create newsletters.

test_reader_cannot_create_newsletter()¶

Readers should NOT be able to create newsletters.

class IuliaBreakingNews.tests.PublisherTests(methodName='runTest')¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for publisher team management via API.

setUp()¶

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_all_roles_can_view_publishers()¶

All authenticated users can view the publisher list.

test_editor_can_join_publisher()¶

Editors should be able to join a publisher’s team.

test_journalist_can_join_publisher()¶

Journalists should be able to join a publisher’s team.

test_journalist_can_leave_publisher()¶

Journalists should be able to leave a publisher’s team.

test_journalist_cannot_manage_publishers_team()¶

Journalists should NOT be able to manage a publisher’s team.

test_publisher_can_manage_team()¶

Publisher should be able to update their team via API.

test_reader_cannot_join_publisher()¶

Readers should NOT be able to join a publisher’s team.

class IuliaBreakingNews.tests.RoleBasedAccessTests(methodName='runTest')¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for role-based permissions on article endpoints.

setUp()¶

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_all_roles_can_view_articles()¶

All authenticated roles should be able to view articles.

test_editor_can_approve_article()¶

Editors should be able to approve articles.

test_editor_can_delete_article()¶

Editors should be able to delete articles.

test_editor_cannot_create_article()¶

Editors should NOT be able to POST articles.

test_journalist_can_create_article()¶

Journalists should be able to POST new articles.

test_reader_cannot_approve_article()¶

Readers should NOT be able to approve articles.

test_reader_cannot_create_article()¶

Readers should NOT be able to POST articles.

test_reader_cannot_delete_article()¶

Readers should NOT be able to delete articles.

class IuliaBreakingNews.tests.SignalTests(methodName='runTest')¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for signal logic on article approval. Uses mocking to avoid real emails and HTTP requests.

setUp()¶

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_email_sent_to_subscribers_on_approval(mock_mail, mock_requests_post)¶

Subscribers should receive an email when article approved.

test_signal_does_not_fire_when_not_approved(mock_email, mock_post)¶

Saving an unapproved article should NOT trigger email or API post.

test_signal_fires_on_article_approval(mock_email, mock_post)¶

When an article is approved, both email and API post functions should be called.

class IuliaBreakingNews.tests.SubscriptionTests(methodName='runTest')¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for reader subscription-based article filtering.

setUp()¶

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_reader_with_no_subscriptions_sees_empty_list()¶

A reader with no subscriptions sees an empty list.

test_subscribed_endpoint_returns_only_subscribed_articles()¶

Reader should only see articles from subscribed sources.

test_subscribed_includes_publisher_articles()¶

Reader subscribed to a publisher sees its articles.

IuliaBreakingNews.tests.auth_client(token)¶

Return an APIClient with token authentication set.

IuliaBreakingNews.tests.create_user(username, role, password='TestPass123!')¶

Helper to create a user and return them with a token.

IuliaBreakingNews.tests.make_article(author, title='Test Article', approved=True)¶

Helper to create an article with default content.

IuliaBreakingNews.urls module¶

URL patterns for the IuliaBreakingNews web application. All routes are included in the main urls.py.

IuliaBreakingNews.views module¶

IuliaBreakingNews Views

Module

This module defines the view logic for the IuliaBreakingNews Django application. It includes views for displaying news articles, managing user interactions, and handling API endpoints for external data consumption.

Dependencies:

  • Django’s authentication system

  • Custom decorators for role-based access control

IuliaBreakingNews.views.approve_article(request, pk)¶

Approve an article (editors only).

IuliaBreakingNews.views.article_create(request)¶

Create a new article (journalists only).

IuliaBreakingNews.views.article_delete(request, pk)¶

Delete an article (editors and journalists).

IuliaBreakingNews.views.article_detail(request, pk)¶

Display a single article.

IuliaBreakingNews.views.article_edit(request, pk)¶

Edit an existing article (editors and journalists).

IuliaBreakingNews.views.article_list(request)¶

Display all approved articles.

IuliaBreakingNews.views.join_publisher(request, pk)¶

Journalist or editor requests to join a publisher’s team. Publisher can remove them from the team via publisher_team view.

IuliaBreakingNews.views.leave_publisher(request, pk)¶

Journalist or editor leaves a publisher’s team.

IuliaBreakingNews.views.login_view(request)¶

Login an existing user.

IuliaBreakingNews.views.logout_view(request)¶

Logout the current user.

IuliaBreakingNews.views.newsletter_create(request)¶

Create a new newsletter (journalists only).

IuliaBreakingNews.views.newsletter_delete(request, pk)¶

Delete a newsletter (editors and journalists).

IuliaBreakingNews.views.newsletter_detail(request, pk)¶

Display a single newsletter.

IuliaBreakingNews.views.newsletter_edit(request, pk)¶

Edit a newsletter (editors and journalists).

IuliaBreakingNews.views.newsletter_list(request)¶

Display all newsletters.

IuliaBreakingNews.views.pending_articles(request)¶

List all articles pending approval (editors only).

IuliaBreakingNews.views.publisher_detail(request, pk)¶

Display a publisher’s profile, team, and articles.

IuliaBreakingNews.views.publisher_list(request)¶

Display all publishers.

IuliaBreakingNews.views.publisher_team(request, pk)¶

Publisher manages their team of journalists and editors. Only the publisher themselves can access this page.

IuliaBreakingNews.views.register_view(request)¶

Register a new user and assign them to the correct group.

IuliaBreakingNews.views.username_recovery(request)¶

Allow users to recover their username via email. Displays the username associated with the provided email.

Module contents¶

IuliaNewsApp
